steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage shows the program was successful: it “could even distinguish between concrete and cobblestone.” For a program to distinguish between different sidewalk materials, it must first successfully locate the sidewalks in the aerial images.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
”Detected” is the word that expresses finding or identifying something in data — the core task of a computer vision program analyzing aerial images. The other choices don't work: “approved” has no relevance to image analysis, “neglected” contradicts the shown success, and “mistook” contradicts both “accurately” and the demonstrated distinction-making.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
